zh.lifeSummary: 當John Dean於1596年出生在英國英格蘭奧可福爾卡維沙安時，他的父親John Deane27歲，母親Joan Fuller22歲。他於1622年8月22日與Susanna Fuller結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1643年6月19日在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可沃特托享年47歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭波克夏卡維沙安。

nameMeanings.name: Dean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

variants.name: Bishop
meanings: English: from Middle English bissop, biscop, Old English bisc(e)op ‘bishop’, which comes via Latin from Greek episkopos ‘overseer’. The Greek word was adopted early in the Christian era as a title for an overseer of a local community of Christians, and has yielded cognates in every European language: French évêque, Italian vescovo, Spanish obispo, Russian yepiskop, German Bischof, etc. The word came to be applied as a surname for a variety of reasons, among them a supposed resemblance in bearing or appearance to a bishop, and selection as the ‘boy bishop’ on Saint Nicholas's Feast Day. In some instances the surname is from the rare Middle English (Old English) personal name Biscop ‘bishop’. As an Irish surname it is adopted for Mac Giolla Easpaig, meaning ‘servant of the bishop’ (see Gillespie ). In North America, this surname has absorbed, by assimilation and translation, at least some of continental European cognates, e.g. German Bischoff , Polish, Rusyn, Czech, and Slovak Biskup , Slovenian Škof (see Skoff ).

variants.name: Denman
meanings: English: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller in the valley’ from Middle English dene + man (see Dean 1).

variants.name: Denson
meanings: patronymic meaning ‘son of the dean’, see Dean 2. Since medieval deans were celibate, their sons would have to have been illegitimate, but le Dene was probably also used as a non-clerical nickname, so illegitimacy is not necessarily to be inferred.
patronymic meaning ‘son of Deyne or Dene’, a pet form of Reynald (Reginald); see Dean 3.

variants.name: Deen
meanings: Dutch: ethnic name for a Dane, Middle Dutch Deen. Alternatively, from the personal name Deen, based on Old High German degan ‘squire, nobleman, thane’.

variants.name: Denton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called. The vast majority, including those in Cambridgeshire, Cumbria, Dumfries, Durham, Kent, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northumberland, Oxfordshire, Sussex, and Yorkshire are named from Old English denu ‘valley’ (see Dean 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. An isolated example in Northamptonshire appears in the Domesday Book as Dodintone meaning ‘enclosure, settlement associated with Dodda or Dudda’.

variants.name: Dann
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), which is often found as dane in placenames in Essex, Hertfordshire, Bedfordshire, Kent, and Sussex. Compare Dean .
English: perhaps from a pet form of the Middle English personal name   Daniel  .
German: topographic name for someone who lived by a pine tree, from Old High German tanna ‘pine tree’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Deaner
meanings: English (Worcestershire): topographic name for someone who lived in a valley, from Middle English dene ‘(thickly wooded) valley’ (see Dean 1) + the suffix -er, denoting an inhabitant.
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Diener  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When John Dean was born in 1596, in Caversham, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Deane, was 27 and his mother, Joan Fuller, was 22. He married Susanna Fuller on 22 August 1622. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 19 June 1643, in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 47, and was buried in Caversham, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Susanna Fuller le 22 août 1622. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 19 juin 1643, à Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Caversham, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: John Dean(이)가 1596년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 카버샤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Deane(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Fuller(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1622년 8월 22일 Susanna Fuller(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1643년 6월 19일 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 왓르타운에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 카버샤에 묻혔다.

ru.Dedham, Bucksport, Lincoln, Massachusett: дедхам, буккспорт, линколн, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Dean родился в 1596 г. в местности кавиршам, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Deane было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Fuller, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Susanna Fuller 22 августа 1622 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 19 июня 1643 г. в местности вэитиртоун, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности кавиршам, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
